Unfortunately, I am unable to verify information about Stegitris lasianthus, so I'll provide general information on a plant with a similar name, Lasianthus (Stegitris doesn't seem to be a valid name, possibly it's a mix of the two), and provide information about Lasianthus lasianthus.

Lasianthus lasianthus, a type of shrub or small tree, is a plant species in the family Theaceae. It is rich in various bioactive constituents, including alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been found to have potential health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, and its extracts have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lasianthus lasianthus include teas, infusions, and dried extracts that are used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections.
